answer: Yes — 2 loans in the 2025-01-01 snapshot of `loan1` carry interest rates outside the 2.0%-18.0% policy band: one at 0.85% (loan_id 10247) and one at 19.40% (loan_id 10193). Both originated through manual underwriter overrides without a policy-exception attribute, so they're flagged by rule DQ-LOAN-001 (Validity, severity=high). Business risk is two-fold: (1) HMDA disparate-impact testing is skewed by the artificially low rate, and (2) the high-rate loan may breach state-level rate-cap regulations and trigger consumer-redress exposure. Recommend immediate review by the pricing-exception committee and a structural fix that requires capture of an exception_code at booking time.
